chunk_summary: A staged work-up for non-response in moderate acute malnutrition includes high-yield tests at 2–4 weeks and targeted tests at 4–8 weeks, guided by clinical findings and specific guidelines for celiac disease, TB, and other conditions.
learning_objectives: Understand the staged investigation strategy for non-response in moderate acute malnutrition.
Identify appropriate high-yield tests for initial evaluation at 2–4 weeks of non-response.
Apply targeted diagnostic guidelines for celiac disease, TB, and other conditions at 4–8 weeks.
Recognize when to escalate care or refer for comprehensive evaluation.
key_facts: Stage A tests (2–4 weeks): haemoglobin, stool examination, and urinalysis with culture.
Stage B tests (4–8 weeks): IgA anti-tTG for celiac disease (children >2 years), IgG-based tests for IgA deficiency, and TB diagnostics per NTEP guidelines.
Persistent non-response beyond 8 weeks warrants referral to a higher facility.
Chest radiographs are recommended only when respiratory symptoms suggest TB or lower respiratory disease.
HIV testing should be considered with recurrent infections or persistent poor response.
reference_quote: For suspected celiac disease, follow ICMR guidance that IgA anti-tTG is the preferred serologic test in children above 2 years.
document_key_topics: Staged diagnostic approach for non-response in MAM
High-yield and targeted tests for common medical causes
Guidelines for celiac disease and TB diagnostics
